description: Trigger discrimination for the Phase 6 reliability cluster: reliability design vs observability implementation vs incident procedures, and against the shipped qa-strategy-architect and saas-cost-architect.

cases.prompt: Set reliability targets and error budgets for our core journeys, and rationalize what pages versus what tickets.
cases.reason: Journey-derived targets, budgets, and the paging policy are reliability design — the spec the operator implements and the runbook's procedures hang off.

cases.prompt: Implement the alert rules from the reliability doc and verify the SLI queries return data.
cases.reason: Implementing rules and executing verification queries is the operator's manual-only work; the design already exists.

cases.prompt: Which product risks should drive our testing investment, and what layers should cover them before release?
cases.reason: Pre-release test-layer investment is QA strategy; SLOs govern the running system's reliability promise — related risk thinking, different artifact.

cases.prompt: Define budgets and alerts for our per-tenant AI spend so a runaway tenant can't blow up the bill.
cases.reason: Cost guardrails and per-tenant spend budgets are the cost model's territory — 'budget' there is dollars, not error budget.

cases.prompt: When the fast-burn alert pages someone at 3am, what exactly should they do? Write that up.
cases.reason: The human procedure behind the page is the incident runbook; this skill only defines when the page fires.
